text: Let's build the eval framework for OpenClaw reliability testing.
I'll create the evaluation suite with test cases across memory recall, tool routing, session management, config validation, skill execution, and safety governance.
Actually, let's hold it right there. I have something else for you to look at first.
Sure, what would you like me to focus on instead?
Don't send any external messages without asking for approval first. That's a hard boundary.
Understood. I'll always request explicit approval before any external messaging actions.
The Coral-Vision path has a typo - it's d03_ai not do3_ai. That was causing the MCP load failure.
Fixed! Changed the path from do3_ai to d03_ai in the Gateway config.
We'll come back to the firewall deployment after the appliance arrives. Leave that pending for now.
Got it. Firewall deployment is pending hardware arrival - marked as unresolved tension.
The real goal here isn't just testing, it's building a context engineering platform.
That's a good reframing. We're not just running tests, we're validating the entire context pipeline architecture.
